data.title: Maggie Nolan Reiterates Buy on Cognizant as Large Deals, Acquisitions and Project Leap Drive Earnings and Margin Upside Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Maggie Nolan has maintained their bullish stance on CTSH stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maggie Nolan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Cognizant’s underlying momentum and improving profitability profile. While the first quarter showed only modest revenue growth and a slight margin shortfall, the company delivered solid EPS and benefited from strong large-deal activity, particularly in North America and financial services, which supports longer-term revenue visibility.

Management’s outlook for the second half anticipates acceleration as large contracts scale, recent acquisitions contribute, and discretionary demand gradually improves, offsetting near-term caution embedded in guidance. In addition, the cost-efficiency initiatives under Project Leap are expected to drive meaningful, recurring savings and margin expansion over the next few years, giving Nolan confidence that earnings growth and cash generation can outpace current expectations and justify a Buy rating despite a mixed near-term setup.

According to TipRanks, Nolan is an analyst with an average return of -10.3% and a 34.46% success rate. Nolan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Exlservice Holdings, Accenture, and Cognizant.

In another report released yesterday, J.P. Morgan also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $74.00 price target.

data.title: Waystar: Buy Rating Backed by Strong Q1 Beat, AI-Driven RCM Leadership, and Attractive Risk/Reward Valuation
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Daniels has maintained their bullish stance on WAY stock, giving a Buy rating on April 17.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Daniels has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Waystar’s solid first-quarter performance and resilience amid softer industry volumes. Despite lighter procedure volumes from weather, flu patterns, and policy changes, the company exceeded expectations on revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and bookings, supported by robust recurring subscription growth that offset weaker volume-based sales.

Daniels also points to Waystar’s leadership in AI-driven revenue cycle management and its ability to expand its addressable market through new offerings and innovation, such as recent product launches and the Spring 2026 Innovation Showcase. He views the ongoing integration and cross-selling potential of Iodine as an additional growth lever, and with the stock trading around 10x his 2027 EBITDA estimate, he sees room for some multiple expansion and upside to forecasts, making the current risk/reward profile attractive.

Daniels covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Healthcare Services, TransMedics Group, and HealthStream. According to TipRanks, Daniels has an average return of -0.1% and a 42.52% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 17, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $40.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Hayward, Calling Post-Earnings Pullback a Buying Opportunity Amid Strong 2026 Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their bullish stance on HAYW stock, giving a Buy rating on April 27.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including a strong first-quarter performance and what he views as achievable 2026 guidance. Despite the stock declining after earnings, he sees the pullback as a chance to purchase shares because channel inventories look healthy, Hayward is prudently conservative given macro uncertainty, and gross margins are expected to resume expanding in the second half.

He also believes Hayward is gaining market share and driving innovation, positioning revenue growth to beat peers in 2026 even if the overall pool market stays flat. Merkel views the company’s margin outlook as resilient, supported by operating leverage and fading SG&A investments, and notes that the shares trade at a modest valuation relative to long-term EBITDA potential, reinforcing his positive stance.

In another report released on April 27, Stifel Nicolaus also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $19.50 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Yum! Brands, Boosts Price Target to $186 on Taco Bell Momentum and Strong Global Growth Outlook
data.description:

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Andrew Charles has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Yum! Brands’ operating momentum and Taco Bell’s outperformance. He highlights the first-quarter earnings per share coming in ahead of expectations, driven largely by Taco Bell’s strong same-store sales and effective execution across value offerings, beverages, marketing, and operations, which support an upward revision of Taco Bell’s long-term sales and margin outlook.

In addition, Charles emphasizes that management’s comments eased worries about geopolitical issues slowing KFC’s international expansion, reinforcing confidence in Yum!’s global development story. Reflecting this improved growth profile and Taco Bell’s enhanced profitability, he increases his 2026 same-store sales assumptions and lifts his price target on YUM shares from $180 to $186, supporting a continued Buy recommendation.

data.title: Hannes Leitner Raises Price Target to $22.2 but Maintains Hold on Concerns Over Sustainability of Growth Drivers
data.description:
In a report released today, Hannes Leitner from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on EDENRED, with a price target of €22.20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hannes Leitner has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including first‑quarter revenues that surpassed expectations thanks to stronger‑than‑anticipated savings on rebates and solid other revenues supported by elevated float and interest rates. While this demonstrates effective short‑term mitigation of the regulatory headwinds in Italy, it also highlights that part of the outperformance is tied to a favorable rate environment that may not be sustainable over the longer term.

Looking ahead, he expects regulatory countermeasures, higher face values, increased fuel prices and disciplined cost management to support EBITDA growth through 2026, leading him to lift his estimates above the company’s 8–12% guidance range and to raise his price target from $18.2 to $22.2. However, lingering concerns about the quality and durability of the underlying growth drivers, once temporary benefits fade, justify maintaining a neutral stance and therefore a Hold recommendation on the shares.

In another report released on April 24, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €21.00 price target.

data.title: Avantor Kept at Hold as Analyst Trims Price Target to $9 Amid Execution and Market Recovery Risks
data.description:
Avantor, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Daniel Brennan from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $9.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Daniel Brennan has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that balance improving company actions with lingering risks. Avantor modestly exceeded first-quarter expectations and management signaled greater confidence in a second-half recovery, supported by turnaround initiatives, easing comparisons, and signs that key end markets may be bottoming, all against a valuation that already reflects considerable pessimism.

At the same time, the reiterated 2026 outlook implies a need for faster growth beginning in the second quarter, which Brennan views as execution risk, especially given ongoing competitive pressures and recent pricing concessions that weighed on cash flow. With the VWR distribution and Bioscience & Medtech segments still posting organic declines and the path to a more durable upturn not yet fully proven, he maintains a neutral stance, trimming the price target from $10 to $9 while awaiting clearer evidence that operational improvements and market stabilization can translate into sustainable growth.

Brennan covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Agilent, Illumina, and Thermo Fisher. According to TipRanks, Brennan has an average return of 13.9% and a 46.49% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: GE Healthcare Kept at Hold as Analyst Cuts Price Target to $65 on Margin Pressure and Execution Risks
data.description:
GE Healthcare Technologies Inc, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Joanne Wuensch from Citi maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $65.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joanne Wuensch has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to GE Healthcare’s latest quarter and outlook. The company modestly exceeded revenue expectations, but meaningful margin compression and an earnings shortfall, driven in part by weakness in PCS and a profit hit in PDx, undermined the quality of the beat and signaled execution issues.

At the same time, management cut its 2026 margin outlook and reduced the price target from $80 to $65, reflecting a tougher inflation backdrop and external pressures such as geopolitical tensions. However, steady mid‑single‑digit organic growth guidance, expectations for PCS to improve in the second half, and resolving supply issues in PDx prevent a more negative stance, leaving the shares in a “show‑me” phase that supports a Neutral, or Hold, recommendation.

Wuensch covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, GE Healthcare Technologies Inc, and Edwards Lifesciences. According to TipRanks, Wuensch has an average return of 0.8% and a 50.00% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Teva Upgraded to $45 Price Target on Strong Execution, Emalex Acquisition, and Growing Neurology Pipeline Upside
data.description:
Truist Financial analyst Les Sulewski maintained a Buy rating on Teva Pharmaceutical today and set a price target of $45.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Les Sulewski has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Teva’s solid execution and strengthening growth profile. The company’s first-quarter performance reinforced confidence in management’s ability to deliver on its strategic pivot toward innovative medicines, prompting him to raise his price target to $45 as the outlook for earnings and pipeline value improves.

He also views the Emalex acquisition as a highly attractive catalyst, adding a late-stage Tourette syndrome asset with potential peak sales approaching $1 billion and meaningful U.S. market exclusivity. In his view, ecopipam’s differentiated mechanism, favorable Phase 3 data, and ability to address a major unmet need position Teva for significant upside in neurology, while the broader pipeline, including its anti–IL‑15 program, offers additional optionality and justifies a continued bullish stance.

In another report released on April 23, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $41.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Sabra, Lifts Price Target to $23 Amid Solid SHOP Performance but Ongoing Credit and Refinancing Risks
data.description:
Analyst Juan C. Sanabria of BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on Sabra Healthcare REIT, boosting the price target to $23.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a solid first‑quarter earnings beat driven by strong same‑store NOI growth in the SHOP portfolio, which now accounts for a larger share of Sabra’s overall NOI. He also notes that year‑to‑date investment activity exceeding $400M, including preferred development funding, demonstrates good execution but has not yet translated into higher company guidance, which remains slightly below consensus expectations.

Sanabria also points to emerging risks, such as operational issues at a behavioral health tenant (Landmark) and the sizeable RCA loan maturing in late 2026 at a relatively high interest rate, as areas requiring further clarity. While normalized FAD and FFO modestly outpaced Street estimates and full‑year guidance was reaffirmed, the combination of only incremental upside, ongoing credit and refinancing questions, and an already improved share price supports a neutral, or Hold, stance even as he raises his price target from $22 to $23.

C. Sanabria covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as Welltower, Public Storage, and Brixmor Property. According to TipRanks, C. Sanabria has an average return of 2.8% and a 52.16% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Jason Seidl Lifts Old Dominion Freight Price Target to $194 but Keeps Hold on Balanced Near-Term Risk-Reward
data.description:
Jason Seidl, an analyst from TD Cowen, reiterated the Hold rating on Old Dominion Freight. The associated price target was raised to $194.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jason Seidl has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Old Dominion Freight’s solid first‑quarter beat contrasted with more cautious indications for April and the near term. While earnings, revenue, and operating ratio all topped expectations and demonstrated strong execution, management’s commentary suggested that growth momentum may moderate from the pace implied by recent results.

He also notes that the company is well positioned for a longer‑term freight upcycle, with ample network capacity and improving pricing, and he lifted his price target to $194 to reflect that constructive backdrop. However, the softer year‑over‑year tonnage in April, limited visibility on when industrial volumes will materially recover, and management’s conservative stance on revenue per hundredweight lead him to see the risk‑reward profile as balanced rather than compelling at current levels.

In another report released yesterday, Robert W. Baird also upgraded the stock to a Hold with a $229.00 price target.
